rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., LFP claim), and proposes minimal repairs (e.g., ablation study).
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence and missing evidence (e.g., uncertainty about specific cell voltages, standard reviewer asks).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed dimensions, addressing logical consistency, uncertainty disclosure, scope adherence, downstream-harm avoidance, and provenance and traceability.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., internal inconsistency) and suggests concrete revisions to address them.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (e.g., specific claims about LFP, standard reviewer asks).
